description: Server-side DOM implementation based on Mozilla's dom.js

vulnerabilities.description: outerHTML setter parses arbitrary HTML via mozHTMLParser without sanitization. Attacker-controlled HTML parsed into DOM tree including <script>, <iframe>, and event handler attributes. Base class innerHTML setter correctly marked utils.nyi (not implemented), but htmlelts.js overrides this on HTMLElement with full HTML parsing.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add HTML sanitization before parsing. Strip <script>, <iframe>, <object>, <embed> tags and event handler attributes.

vulnerabilities.description: HTMLElement.innerHTML setter fully implemented with HTML parsing via mozHTMLParser. Parses arbitrary HTML without sanitization — <script> tags, event handler attributes, and other dangerous content pass through unchecked.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize parsed HTML or document that consumers must sanitize output before serving to browsers.

vulnerabilities.description: Event constructor copies all enumerable properties from dictionary argument via unguarded for...in loop: `for(var p in dictionary) { this[p] = dictionary[p]; }`. If Object.prototype is polluted elsewhere, attacker-controlled properties can inject into Event instances.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace with Object.keys(dictionary).forEach(p => { this[p] = dictionary[p]; }) or whitelist known Event properties.

vulnerabilities.description: dangerouslySetInnerHTML property exists and parses HTML without sanitization. Name mirrors React's API and acknowledges danger but provides no built-in protection. Acts as direct passthrough to innerHTML parsing.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add JSDoc warning about XSS risks. Consider adding sanitization flag to the setter.

vulnerabilities.description: insertAdjacentHTML implementation parses arbitrary HTML via mozHTMLParser without sanitization. Accepts position+text and parses text as HTML, inserting into DOM at specified position. Same XSS risk profile as innerHTML/outerHTML.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize parsed HTML fragment before insertion into DOM tree.

vulnerabilities.description: Location.assign() resolves relative URLs against current href without protocol validation. javascript: and data: URLs pass through unchecked. Server-side only so limited practical impact, but could affect URL resolution in DOM serialization scenarios.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate protocol before navigation. Block javascript: and data: URL schemes.

other.note: Event prototype pollution + Location.js protocol bypass (minor, server-side only)

positiveFindings: Zero production dependencies — no supply chain attack surface from npm packages
No hardcoded secrets, API keys, passwords, or tokens found in any source file
No eval(), new Function(), or dynamic code execution found
No filesystem access (fs module not imported)
No child_process, shell execution, or network calls
Element.prototype.innerHTML setter correctly marked utils.nyi at base class level
HTMLParser uses __proto__: null objects for lookups (null-prototype safety)
Library is server-side only — scripts parsed as DOM nodes but never executed in browser context
dangerouslySetInnerHTML name explicitly signals danger to consumers
DOMTokenList properly validates token strings (no whitespace, non-empty) before operations
riskContext: Domino is a server-side DOM library with ZERO attack surface from dependencies. Primary risk is misuse: if consumer applications parse untrusted HTML via innerHTML/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML setters and serialize DOM back to HTML served to browsers without sanitization, stored/persistent XSS is possible. Library itself does not execute scripts, access filesystem, or make network calls. Risk score 22/100 reflects low inherent risk — medium risk contingent on consumer usage patterns.
recommendations: DOCUMENT: Add prominent security warning in README that innerHTML/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML parse arbitrary HTML without sanitization. Consumers MUST sanitize output before serving to browsers.
SANITIZE: Add optional HTML sanitization mode to HTMLParser that strips <script>, <iframe>, <object>, <embed> tags and on* event attributes when enabled.
HARDEN: Replace for...in loop in Event constructor (Event.js:30) with Object.keys(dictionary).forEach() to prevent prototype pollution risk.
VALIDATE: Add protocol validation in Location.assign() to block javascript: and data: URL schemes.
CONFIG: Add configurable allowlist/blocklist for HTML elements and attributes during parsing.
TEST: Add security test cases verifying that dangerous content in HTML parsing paths is handled appropriately.
MONITOR: Track devDependencies (mocha, puppeteer) for vulnerabilities despite being dev-only.
